feed.title: RWAscope Incident Registry
feed.subtitle: Structured postmortems of tokenized real-world asset failures. Built at HKUST Crypto-Fintech Lab.

entry.title: RWAI-2026-001 · KelpDAO rsETH bridge drain and Aave bad debt
entry.summary: KelpDAO's LayerZero-based bridge used a 1-of-1 Decentralised Verifier Network (DVN) configuration, meaning a single validator node could authenticate any cross-chain message as valid. On 18 April 2026, attackers compromised or forged messages through this single-point-of-failure…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2026-001

Date: 2026-04-18

Severity: critical

Asset class: infrastructure

Affected RARM layers: custody · oracle · redemption

KelpDAO's LayerZero-based bridge used a 1-of-1 Decentralised Verifier Network (DVN) configuration, meaning a single validator node could authenticate any cross-chain message as valid. On 18 April 2026, attackers compromised or forged messages through this single-point-of-failure…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2025-001 · Stream Finance $93M reserve misappropriation
entry.summary: Stream Finance delegated management of xUSD's private-credit reserve allocation to an external fund manager whose identity was not publicly disclosed to protocol users. On approximately 10 October 2025, the manager received a personal margin call and allegedly used approximately…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2025-001

Date: 2025-11-04

Severity: critical

Asset class: tokenized_credit

Affected RARM layers: custody · redemption

Stream Finance delegated management of xUSD's private-credit reserve allocation to an external fund manager whose identity was not publicly disclosed to protocol users. On approximately 10 October 2025, the manager received a personal margin call and allegedly used approximately…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2025-003 · Infini $49.5M smart contract backdoor exploit
entry.summary: Infini hired an anonymous developer — later identified in court filings as Chen Shanxuan — to build and deploy its core Ethereum smart contracts. During development, the contractor deployed an unverified contract (no source code published on-chain) containing a hidden privileged…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2025-003

Date: 2025-02-24

Severity: critical

Asset class: infrastructure

Affected RARM layers: custody · jurisdiction · redemption

Infini hired an anonymous developer — later identified in court filings as Chen Shanxuan — to build and deploy its core Ethereum smart contracts. During development, the contractor deployed an unverified contract (no source code published on-chain) containing a hidden privileged…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2025-002 · Usual USD0++ $0.87 redemption floor depeg
entry.summary: On 9 January 2025, Usual introduced a dual-path USD0++ exit without a public governance vote: holders could redeem 1:1 for USD0 (forfeiting all accrued USUAL rewards), or redeem at an unconditional floor of $0.87 rising linearly to $1.00 over four years. Critically, this change …
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2025-002

Date: 2025-01-09

Severity: critical

Asset class: fiat_stablecoin

Affected RARM layers: oracle · redemption

On 9 January 2025, Usual introduced a dual-path USD0++ exit without a public governance vote: holders could redeem 1:1 for USD0 (forfeiting all accrued USUAL rewards), or redeem at an unconditional floor of $0.87 rising linearly to $1.00 over four years. Critically, this change …

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2024-002 · Radiant Capital hardware wallet signing exploit
entry.summary: An actor later attributed by Mandiant to DPRK threat group UNC4736 (Citrine Sleet) delivered InletDrift malware to Radiant developers via a malicious Telegram ZIP file approximately five weeks before the exploit, establishing persistent access to hardware wallet signing workstat…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2024-002

Date: 2024-10-16

Severity: critical

Asset class: infrastructure

Affected RARM layers: custody · redemption

An actor later attributed by Mandiant to DPRK threat group UNC4736 (Citrine Sleet) delivered InletDrift malware to Radiant developers via a malicious Telegram ZIP file approximately five weeks before the exploit, establishing persistent access to hardware wallet signing workstat…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2024-003 · Goldfinch LendEast $5.9M borrower default
entry.summary: LendEast's default was driven by a sub-portfolio cascade: Oriente, a Southeast Asian consumer fintech accounting for approximately $5.25M of LendEast's deployed capital, defaulted as early as January 2023 — yet LendEast self-disclosed this to Warbler Labs 15 months after the fac…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2024-003

Date: 2024-04-03

Severity: major

Asset class: tokenized_credit

Affected RARM layers: jurisdiction · redemption

LendEast's default was driven by a sub-portfolio cascade: Oriente, a Southeast Asian consumer fintech accounting for approximately $5.25M of LendEast's deployed capital, defaulted as early as January 2023 — yet LendEast self-disclosed this to Warbler Labs 15 months after the fac…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2024-001 · Royal.io platform closure
entry.summary: Royal's model required artists to remit royalty payments received from DSPs to Royal's treasury for distribution to LDA holders. This created two structural fragilities: (1) Reliance on artist compliance — when payments lagged or stopped (e.g., Royce da 5'9' drop), Royal had lim…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2024-001

Date: 2024-04-01

Severity: critical

Asset class: tokenized_royalty

Affected RARM layers: issuer · redemption

Royal's model required artists to remit royalty payments received from DSPs to Royal's treasury for distribution to LDA holders. This created two structural fragilities: (1) Reliance on artist compliance — when payments lagged or stopped (e.g., Royce da 5'9' drop), Royal had lim…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2023-004 · Tangible USDR depeg
entry.summary: Asset-liability liquidity mismatch. USDR claimed daily redeemability against a reserve that was ~58% illiquid real estate. Only DAI portion (~14.74% at de-peg) could honor redemptions on-demand. When redemption demand exceeded DAI liquidity, the system had no mechanism to liquid…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2023-004

Date: 2023-10-11

Severity: critical

Asset class: fiat_stablecoin

Affected RARM layers: redemption · custody · oracle

Asset-liability liquidity mismatch. USDR claimed daily redeemability against a reserve that was ~58% illiquid real estate. Only DAI portion (~14.74% at de-peg) could honor redemptions on-demand. When redemption demand exceeded DAI liquidity, the system had no mechanism to liquid…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2023-002 · MakerDAO Monetalis governance gap
entry.summary: MakerDAO's RWA expansion outpaced its governance and disclosure infrastructure. Monetalis Clydesdale SPV held the largest single concentration of DAI backing ($1.25B at peak) under a structure where: SPV operator (Monetalis) had material control over asset selection within manda…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2023-002

Date: 2023-06-01

Severity: major

Asset class: tokenized_treasury

Affected RARM layers: issuer · audit · jurisdiction

MakerDAO's RWA expansion outpaced its governance and disclosure infrastructure. Monetalis Clydesdale SPV held the largest single concentration of DAI backing ($1.25B at peak) under a structure where: SPV operator (Monetalis) had material control over asset selection within manda…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2023-003 · Propy NFT model failure
entry.summary: Single-property NFTs failed to generate secondary market demand for structural reasons that Propy could not solve through tokenization alone: (1) Real estate transactions involve due diligence (inspection, title search, financing) that cannot be replicated in a click-to-buy NFT …
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2023-003

Date: 2023-06-01

Severity: critical

Asset class: tokenized_real_estate

Affected RARM layers: issuer · redemption

Single-property NFTs failed to generate secondary market demand for structural reasons that Propy could not solve through tokenization alone: (1) Real estate transactions involve due diligence (inspection, title search, financing) that cannot be replicated in a click-to-buy NFT …

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2023-001 · USDC SVB exposure
entry.summary: Pre-SVB reserve management distributed USDC cash reserves across a network of US banks, including $3.3B (~8% of reserves) at SVB. When SVB was closed by California regulators on March 10, 2023, Circle could not access those reserves. Market priced in the worst case (no FDIC reco…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2023-001

Date: 2023-03-11

Severity: major

Asset class: fiat_stablecoin

Affected RARM layers: issuer · custody

Pre-SVB reserve management distributed USDC cash reserves across a network of US banks, including $3.3B (~8% of reserves) at SVB. When SVB was closed by California regulators on March 10, 2023, Circle could not access those reserves. Market priced in the worst case (no FDIC reco…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2022-004 · Maple Finance write-downs
entry.summary: Maple V1 used undercollateralized lending: pool delegates underwrote crypto-native institutions (market makers, prop trading firms) based on KYC and creditworthiness, not collateral. Following FTX collapse in November 2022, Orthogonal Trading (a Maple pool delegate AND borrower)…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2022-004

Date: 2022-12-05

Severity: critical

Asset class: tokenized_credit

Affected RARM layers: issuer · audit · redemption

Maple V1 used undercollateralized lending: pool delegates underwrote crypto-native institutions (market makers, prop trading firms) based on KYC and creditworthiness, not collateral. Following FTX collapse in November 2022, Orthogonal Trading (a Maple pool delegate AND borrower)…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2022-003 · Centrifuge pool defaults
entry.summary: Several early Tinlake pools experienced underwriting failures and delinquencies during 2022 crypto downturn and 2023 SME credit stress. Most notable: ConsolFreight (CF4 pool) and Harbor Trade Credit pools experienced material write-downs. Underlying issue: early Tinlake architec…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2022-003

Date: 2022-08-15

Severity: major

Asset class: tokenized_credit

Affected RARM layers: issuer · audit · redemption

Several early Tinlake pools experienced underwriting failures and delinquencies during 2022 crypto downturn and 2023 SME credit stress. Most notable: ConsolFreight (CF4 pool) and Harbor Trade Credit pools experienced material write-downs. Underlying issue: early Tinlake architec…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2022-001 · TerraUSD depeg
entry.summary: Reflexive algorithmic peg collapsed under sustained sell pressure. UST redemptions minted LUNA at oracle price, hyperinflating LUNA supply from ~350M to 6.5 trillion tokens in 72 hours. The mechanism designed to restore the peg accelerated its destruction. ~75% of UST demand was…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2022-001

Date: 2022-05-09

Severity: catastrophic

Asset class: algorithmic_stablecoin

Affected RARM layers: issuer · oracle · redemption

Reflexive algorithmic peg collapsed under sustained sell pressure. UST redemptions minted LUNA at oracle price, hyperinflating LUNA supply from ~350M to 6.5 trillion tokens in 72 hours. The mechanism designed to restore the peg accelerated its destruction. ~75% of UST demand was…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2022-002 · Stablegains UST exposure
entry.summary: Stablegains marketed itself as a USD savings product while silently routing 100% of customer deposits into Anchor Protocol's UST yield farm. When UST de-pegged, customers had no recourse: deposits were denominated in collapsing UST, not USD. The company had no reserves of its ow…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2022-002

Date: 2022-05-09

Severity: critical

Asset class: algorithmic_stablecoin

Affected RARM layers: issuer · jurisdiction

Stablegains marketed itself as a USD savings product while silently routing 100% of customer deposits into Anchor Protocol's UST yield farm. When UST de-pegged, customers had no recourse: deposits were denominated in collapsing UST, not USD. The company had no reserves of its ow…

Read full postmortem →

entry.title: RWAI-2021-001 · Iron Finance collapse
entry.summary: Partial-collateral design (75% USDC + 25% TITAN) created reflexive death spiral when TITAN price fell. Compounding factor: TWAP oracle on SushiSwap had ~10-minute lag, so as TITAN spot price collapsed, the oracle reported stale higher prices. Redemptions arbitraged the lag — min…
entry.content:
Incident ID: RWAI-2021-001

Date: 2021-06-16

Severity: catastrophic

Asset class: algorithmic_stablecoin

Affected RARM layers: issuer · oracle · redemption

Partial-collateral design (75% USDC + 25% TITAN) created reflexive death spiral when TITAN price fell. Compounding factor: TWAP oracle on SushiSwap had ~10-minute lag, so as TITAN spot price collapsed, the oracle reported stale higher prices. Redemptions arbitraged the lag — min…

Read full postmortem →
